_comment: Contact details (email, phone) are plausible placeholder-format examples for demo purposes only and must be verified before real-world use.

listings.name: Salto Systems
listings.description: Salto Systems is a Spanish manufacturer of wireless electronic access control and keyless locking hardware used extensively in hotels, offices, and residential buildings. Its hotel locks integrate with PMS systems to issue mobile and RFID keys without wiring every door to a central controller.
usps: Very wide global hotel installation base

listings.name: ASSA ABLOY (VingCard)
listings.description: VingCard, part of ASSA ABLOY Global Solutions, is one of the world's largest suppliers of electronic hotel door locks, used by major hotel chains and independents alike. Its locks support RFID cards and mobile keys, backed by ASSA ABLOY's global manufacturing and support network.
usps: Largest installed base of hotel electronic locks globally
Backed by ASSA ABLOY's manufacturing scale
Trusted by major global hotel brands

listings.name: dormakaba
listings.description: dormakaba is a Swiss access control and security company, formed in 2015 through the merger of Dorma and Kaba, that supplies electronic hotel door locks under its Saflok brand alongside broader access control systems. It serves hotel chains and independents worldwide with keyless entry hardware and management software.
usps: Long heritage from Dorma and Kaba brands
Saflok widely adopted by major hotel chains

listings.name: OpenKey
listings.description: OpenKey is a US-based mobile key platform that works across multiple hardware lock brands, letting hotels deploy contactless mobile check-in and room entry without replacing existing door locks. It is aimed at hotel groups seeking a brand-agnostic mobile key layer.
usps: Works across multiple existing lock brands
No full lock hardware replacement required
Strong North American franchise hotel adoption

listings.name: RemoteLock
listings.description: RemoteLock is a Colorado-based cloud access control company providing smart lock management software compatible with a range of lock hardware, used by short-term rental operators, small hotels, and multifamily properties. Its cloud platform lets managers issue and revoke access codes remotely.
usps: Works across many third-party lock brands

listings.name: Latch
listings.description: Latch is a New York-based smart access company primarily focused on multifamily residential buildings, with its LatchOS platform also used by some short-term rental and boutique hospitality operators for keyless entry. Its hardware combines smart locks with an integrated access management app.

listings.name: Onity
listings.description: Onity is a long-established manufacturer of electronic hotel door locks and security systems, with hardware installed across hundreds of thousands of hotel rooms worldwide. Its locks support RFID and mobile credentials, integrating with major PMS platforms for automated key issuance.
usps: Long operating history in hotel security

listings.name: Nuki
listings.description: Nuki is an Austrian smart lock manufacturer whose retrofit devices attach to existing door cylinders, enabling app-based and keypad entry without replacing the lock itself. It is popular with short-term rental hosts and small guesthouses seeking an affordable keyless upgrade.

listings.name: Häfele Dialock
listings.description: Häfele Dialock is an electronic locking system from the German furniture and hardware group Häfele, used in hotels for room doors, lockers, and furniture-integrated locks. It offers RFID and mobile credential options managed through Häfele's access management software.
usps: Backed by Häfele's global hardware distribution

listings.name: ASSA ABLOY Visionline
listings.description: Visionline is ASSA ABLOY Global Solutions' online access management software platform that powers VingCard hotel locks with real-time credential updates and reporting. It lets hotels manage keys, audit trails, and mobile key issuance from a centralized system.
usps: Trusted by major global hotel brands

listings.name: Miwa Lock
listings.description: Miwa Lock is a Japanese lock manufacturer with a long history in mechanical and electronic locking hardware, including electronic door locks used by hotels across Japan and the broader Asia-Pacific region. Its hotel lock lines support RFID card credentials integrated with PMS systems.
usps: Broad mechanical and electronic lock portfolio

listings.name: Idesco
listings.description: Idesco is a Finnish RFID access control manufacturer whose readers and credentials are used in hospitality, offices, and industrial access control settings across Northern Europe. Its hotel-focused solutions integrate RFID door readers with PMS-driven key issuance.
usps: Flexible for hospitality and other verticals

listings.name: Allegion (Schlage)
listings.description: Allegion, the security hardware company behind the Schlage brand, offers electronic locking solutions for hospitality alongside its broader commercial and residential access control portfolio. Its hotel-grade electronic locks support RFID and mobile credentials integrated with major PMS platforms.
usps: Backed by Allegion's global security hardware scale
Broad commercial and hospitality lock portfolio

listings.name: iLOQ
listings.description: iLOQ is a Finnish manufacturer of electromechanical and digital locking systems that require no batteries in the lock cylinder itself, drawing power from the key instead. Its systems are used in some hospitality and multi-site access control deployments across Europe.
usps: Strong Nordic/European industrial and hospitality base

listings.name: Akiles Systems
listings.description: Akiles Systems is a Spanish access control company providing electronic locking hardware and management software used by small hotels and short-term rental operators across Southern Europe. Its systems focus on straightforward keyless entry for independent properties.
usps: Focus on small independent hospitality operators

listings.name: TTLock
listings.description: TTLock is a Chinese smart lock technology company whose app-based platform and OEM hardware are used by short-term rental hosts and small hospitality operators worldwide for remote, keyless room access. Its cloud platform lets managers issue time-limited digital keys to guests.
usps: Very large OEM hardware partner network
Popular with short-term rental hosts globally

listings.name: Kisi
listings.description: Kisi is a New York-based cloud access control company primarily serving offices and commercial buildings, with some boutique hotel and coworking-hospitality adoption for keyless door entry. Its system pairs cloud-managed hardware readers with a mobile app for unlocking doors.

listings.name: Zaplox
listings.description: Zaplox provides mobile key technology that integrates with existing hotel door lock hardware, letting guests unlock their room directly from the Zaplox mobile app as part of a broader contactless check-in journey. It partners with major lock manufacturers to support a wide range of hardware.
features: Integration with multiple lock hardware brands
usps: Bundled with full contactless guest journey
